mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth rate is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near its effective lower bound, it signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which typically encourages risk-taking and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales growth and low inflation suggests robust consumer demand without overheating, allowing for sustained economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 3%
failure_condition: If the specified conditions are met, but the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, any significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving economic conditions and increased consumer confidence, which typically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as recovery takes hold over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines as specified during a post-recession recovery, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in housing starts indicates weakening demand and potential economic contraction, leading to concerns about future corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average declines by > 10% from its 12-month peak
failure_condition: If HOUST declines as specified, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects robust manufacturing and overall economic activity, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO 3-month annualized growth is positive, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment environment, if the Federal Reserve maintains a low federal funds rate, it signals a commitment to stimulating the economy, which supports asset prices and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% while UNRATE is above 7.0%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in real GDP indicates a contracting economy, which directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 shows negative growth for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over any 5-year rolling window.
